items.body: E-ink's 200 ms redraw feels slow next to OLED, but on a wall display nothing else looks this much like paper in real sunlight. The trade is deliberate: you pay once in refresh latency and buy back readability at every angle, every lighting condition, every glance from across the room.

Floyd–Steinberg still wins at 1:1 scale on 3-bit panels: diffuse the quantisation error, get smooth greys for free. No neural net, no training data, no drift. Just a 1970s algorithm that still survives every benchmark a modern paper throws at it, because the problem it solves hasn't changed.
